architecture_summary: Extend the existing TemporalRetentionWindow receipt instead of introducing a second approval module. The policy defines when retention approval and backup guard evidence are required, the request carries approval refs, approval certificate hash, and backup guard hash, and the evaluator blocks due or overdue destructive/high-risk actions when that evidence is absent. Retention-active records remain deferred by timing; low-risk not-required records remain non-authorizing.
data_model_changes: add retention approval policy fields for destructive actions, high-risk actions, minimum approval refs, and backup guard hash requirement
add retention approval refs, approval certificate hash, and backup guard hash to TemporalRetentionRequest
add retention approval required flag, retained approval refs, approval certificate hash, backup guard hash, and approval metadata to TemporalRetentionWindowReceipt
rollback_plan: Remove the retention approval fields and helpers from gateway/temporal_retention_window.py, remove the corresponding schema fields and conditionals, remove the focused approval-boundary test and SDLC artifacts, then rerun temporal retention tests, schema validation, SDLC artifact validation, security review validation, release readiness validation, and workspace governance preflight.
migration_plan: No persistence migration is required. Existing callers constructing TemporalRetentionRequest without approval fields get default empty approval evidence and will fail closed only when a destructive or high-risk lifecycle action is due or overdue and policy requires approval.
